howItWorks.intro: This energy independence score by country uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Score factors in domestic production, reserves, renewable share, and strategic stockpiles
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: Is the US energy independent?
faq.answer: The US became a net petroleum exporter in 2019 for the first time since the 1950s, thanks to the shale revolution. It scores high on energy independence but still imports specific crude grades and is affected by global pricing.

faq.question: Why does energy independence matter?
faq.answer: Energy dependent nations are vulnerable to supply disruptions, price manipulation by exporters, and geopolitical coercion. Countries like Japan import 97% of their oil, making them extremely vulnerable to Middle East instability.

faq.question: Is the Energy Independence Score by Country free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard geopolitics and energy markets formulas trusted by analysts and informed citizens. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What country should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate country value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Energy Independence Score by Country?
sections.body: The Energy Independence Score by Country is a free online tool designed for analysts and informed citizens who need quick, accurate calculations in the geopolitics and energy markets space. By entering your country, you get instant results including independence score, domestic production, domestic consumption. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting independence score right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In geopolitics and energy markets, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by analysts and informed citizens worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to understand global dynamics with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful for quick estimates before committing to a decision. It's also great to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Country
howToSteps.description: Type or select your country in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your independence score, domestic production, domestic consumption. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your country before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both independence score and domestic production — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to understand global dynamics.
